title: Vegan banana bread
subtitle: Oil-based loaf with no eggs or dairy
excerpt: A moist, banana-forward quick bread made without eggs or dairy. Ripe bananas provide sweetness and bind the loaf; vegetable oil replaces butter. One bowl, forty-minute bake.
sourceNotes: Vegan banana bread adapts the American quick-bread formula by replacing eggs with extra ripe banana and using oil in place of butter. The banana provides enough binding and moisture that no egg substitute is required. This version follows the standard one-bowl method.

recipe.freezeNotes: Slice and freeze. Thaw individual slices at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps for 4 days wrapped. Slightly better on day two.

glossaryTerms.definition: Bananas with brown-speckled or fully black skins have converted most of their starch to sugar and have the softest, most mashable flesh. They give banana bread its sweetness and help bind the loaf without eggs. Yellow or underripe bananas will produce a less sweet loaf with a slightly raw banana taste.

content.text: This loaf has no eggs and no dairy, but the technique is the same as any quick bread: wet ingredients into dry, stir until just combined, bake. The key variable is the bananas.
are not optional — they are the binder, the sweetener, and the primary flavour. Do not use yellow bananas.

attrs.prepNote: very ripe (about 300 g mashed), peeled and mashed

content.text: Heat the oven to 160°C fan. Grease and line a 900 g loaf tin.
Mash the {{bananas}} thoroughly in a large bowl until almost smooth — a few lumps are fine. Stir in the {{light-brown-sugar}}, {{vegetable-oil}}, and {{vanilla-extract}}.
Add the {{plain-flour}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{fine-sea-salt}}, and {{ground-cinnamon}}. Stir until just combined — do not overmix. Fold in the {{walnuts}} if using.

Pour the batter into the tin. Bake for 50–55 minutes until a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean. The top will crack along the centre as it bakes. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes before turning out.
